summary: Blazing Not Fade Away > Black Peter sandwich with tight jams
blurb: The second set erupts with a thunderous Not Fade Away → Black Peter sequence and solid first‑set work. Jerry’s soaring leads, Phil’s punchy bass, and Keith’s keyboards drive the high‑energy jams. Highlights include Not Fade Away, Black Peter, Estimated Prophet and Around & Around.
review: Tempe ’77 delivers a classic late‑summer blaze. The band kicks off with a solid first set that moves from The Promised Land to The Music Never Stopped without a hitch, but the real fireworks begin in the second set. A blistering Not Fade Away rolls straight into an unforgettable Black Peter jam before looping back into another Not Fade Away and closing out the night on a rollicking Around & Around. Jerry’s “magic” guitar licks soar over Keith’s bright keyboards while Phil’s bass anchors the groove, and both Mickey and Bill drive the relentless rhythm that fuels the fast‑paced Not Fade Away. The show also shines with a tight Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World transition, making this one of the tighter 1977 second‑set outings. Despite a few recordings suffering from splices or occasional silence, the performance itself is consistently praised across sources, and the best‐sounding capture preserves every nuance of that high‑octane energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of ten recordings are explicitly positive, praising the Not Fade Away/Black Peter sandwich, tight jams and overall energy; four are mixed but contain no consistent criticism of playing. The prevalence of enthusiastic language ("killer", "explosive", "magic guitar") across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent, crisp sound with no drop‑outs; captures the standout Not Fade Away → Black Peter sandwich and the high‑energy Round & Round jam.

key_highlights: Explosive Not Fade Away → Black Peter sandwich that drives the second set
Tight Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World transition
Powerful drumming that propels the fast‑paced jams
Overall high‑energy performance despite occasional recording glitches in other sources

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers magic guitar licks, soaring solos and emotive vocals that lift the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a punchy, prominent bass line that locks in tightly with the drums throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Strong vocal contributions and a memorable “Bobby W. banshee” solo add texture to the performance.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboard work drives the intensity of the second‑set jams, especially during Not Fade Away and Black Peter.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are driving and powerful, adding extra weight to the fast Not Fade Away bursts.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a tight, propulsive groove that underpins the entire set, complementing Mickey’s flair.
